title: StudySpec
description: Structured interpretation of a study's methods, emitted by the Study Interpreter. Consumed by Cohort Builder, Cohort Assembler, and Analyst. Contains NO published result values (those live in target-results.yaml) - this file describes the ANALYSIS, not its answers.

source_read.description: What the Interpreter actually read to build this spec. Drives how much can be reproduced.

data_dictionary_read.description: MUST be true before classes are assigned - classes depend on which variables survive in the public file.

notable_coarsenings.description: Variables the dictionary shows as binned/removed vs. what the paper used (e.g. age binned to categories, center IDs stripped, exact dates removed). Each drives a coarsening-limited class somewhere.

coding.description: How the paper models it, e.g. 'age groups: <60, 60-69, >=70' or 'continuous'

available_in_public_file_as.description: The dictionary column(s) that realize this exposure, or 'binned only' / 'absent'.

structure.description: composite-survival -> KM/log-rank/Cox. competing-risk -> CIF/Gray + Fine-Gray OR cause-specific Cox. Chosen by the estimand, not by preference.

event_definition.description: Which raw component fields assemble the event (e.g. GRFS event = death OR relapse OR grade III-IV aGVHD OR chronic GVHD).

estimator.description: The exact model the paper used for this endpoint, e.g. 'Kaplan-Meier + Cox PH' or 'cumulative incidence + cause-specific Cox'. The Analyst must match this, not substitute a preferred one.

covariates.description: The full adjustment set as stated in the paper's methods.

note.description: e.g. 'paper used continuous, file bins to categories -> continuous model would be coarsening-limited'.

stated_verbatim.description: The methods text the Interpreter is basing this on, quoted, so downstream reasons can cite it.

adjustment_strategy.description: e.g. 'multivariable Cox adjusting for the covariate set above'. If the paper only says 'adjusted for relevant covariates' with no list -> flag as an ambiguity and expect not-reproducible targets.

random_effects.description: e.g. ['center'] -> center random effect. Center IDs are stripped from public files, so any target depending on this is coarsening-limited. Empty for this milestone study (autologous).

ambiguities.description: Anything under-specified in the paper. Each should map to a not-reproducible or coarsening-limited class in target-results.yaml.

operationalization.description: How to realize this in the data dictionary's columns.
